article.description: — Joel Embiid popped up from a pregame fall and scored 27 points with nine rebounds, making 4 of 5 from 3-point range and sinking a pair of free throws with 3 seconds left to turn back Boston’s final rally and lead the Philadelphia 76ers to a 118-114...
article.headline: Embiid shakes off pregame fall, leads Sixers to 118-114 win over Celtics

images.caption: Tyrese Maxey drops 33 points in the 76ers' Christmas Day win over the Celtics.
images.name: Tyrese Maxey powers 76ers past Celtics on Christmas

article.linkText: Embiid shakes off pregame fall, leads Sixers to 118-114 win over Celtics
article.story: BOSTON -- — Joel Embiid popped up from a pregame fall and scored 27 points with nine rebounds, making 4 of 5 from 3-point range and sinking a pair of free throws with 3 seconds left to turn back Boston’s final rally and lead the Philadelphia 76ers to a 118-114 victory on Wednesday. Tyrese Maxey had 33 points and 12 assists. He scored 13 in the fourth quarter, including a quick-trigger layup to open a seven-point lead with about a minute left after the Sixers inbounded the ball with 1.1 seconds on the shot clock. Caleb Martin scored 23, hitting 7 of 9 from 3-point range. Jayson Tatum scored 32 with 15 rebounds for Boston, Jaylen Brown scored 23 and Derrick White had 21. Boston wore red socks and leggings with their green jerseys for an extra dose of Christmas spirit. Celtics center Kristaps Porzingis, who has missed all but nine games this season with injuries, did not return for the second half. The team said he had an ankle injury. Takeaways 76ers: If Embiid stays healthy, the Sixers may finally be able to topple them in the Eastern Conference. Celtics: Lost two in a row for the first time this season. Key moment Embiid fell and grabbed his ankle during pregame warmups, but started and played 31 minutes wearing a black mask to protect his sinus fracture. He limped to the free throw line after he was fouled on a drive to the basket with two minutes left and Philadelphia leading 108-105. He made both free throws, and sank two more with 3 seconds left after the Celtics cut a nine-point lead to 116-114 in the final minute. Key stat The Celtics trailed by as many as 16 points, 52-36, in the third quarter – their largest deficit at home this season. Up next The Sixers visit Utah on Saturday in the second game of a six-game road trip. Boston hosts Indiana on Friday and Sunday. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/hub/nba

images.caption: Victor Wembanyama (center left) and the San Antonio Spurs are favored entering the NBA Finals.
images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images

images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Victor Wembanyama are Nos. 1 and 2 in the Finals MVP odds, respectively.

images.caption: Jalen Brunson has the New York Knicks in the NBA Finals for the first time since 1999.

images.caption: The Knicks are looking to end the series against the Cavaliers tonight, as they have the second-shortest title odds and are an overwhelming favorite to win the East.

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: The Knicks are now heavy favorites to win the Eastern Conference after taking a 3-0 series lead over the Cavs.
images.credit: Nathaniel S. Butler/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.caption: Jamal Murray has led the Nuggets while Nikola Jokic recovers from injury.
